bulkAction.selectAllImagesHint: Select every image in the list (not every tag)
bulkAction.addFrontHint: Prepend the tags above to each selected image's caption

disableHints.lr_scheduler: Adaptive optimizers use a constant learning rate
disableHints.noise_enhancement_type: Disabled while InfoNoise is enabled (schema mutex)
disableHints.loss_weighting: Disabled while InfoNoise is enabled (schema mutex, only `none` compatible)
disableHints.loss_type: Disabled while InfoNoise is enabled (schema mutex, only `mse` compatible)
disableHints.timestep_schedule_shift: Disabled while InfoNoise is enabled (schema mutex, only `1.0` compatible)
disableHints.infonoise_enabled: Reset noise_enhancement / loss_weighting / loss_type / schedule_shift to defaults to enable (schema mutex)

descriptions.data_dir: Dataset directory (supports Kohya-style N_xxx repeat folders)
descriptions.reg_data_dir: Regularization dataset directory (optional, helps prevent overfitting)
descriptions.reg_caption: Shared regularization caption (leave blank to use each image's .txt/.json)
descriptions.reg_weight: Regularization loss weight relative to the main set; 1.0 = equal, lower values weaken reg
descriptions.shuffle_caption: Shuffle tags (JSON mode shuffles within categories, TXT mode shuffles all tags)
descriptions.keep_tokens: Protect the first N tags from shuffling (TXT mode only)
descriptions.tag_dropout: Per-tag random dropout probability during training (0 = off); non-zero values help generalization and reduce single-tag dependence
descriptions.prefer_json: Prefer JSON tag files (recommended; supports category shuffle)
descriptions.cache_latents: Cache VAE latents to speed up training
descriptions.lora_type: Adapter algorithm. lokr: Kronecker factorization, smallest parameter count (default); lora: classic low-rank, general purpose; loha: Hadamard product, higher expressiveness but more parameters
descriptions.lora_rank: Rank: larger = more capacity but higher parameter/VRAM cost and overfit risk; smaller = leaner but underfits more easily. Common 8/16/32/64
descriptions.lora_alpha: Alpha: LoRA scaling factor; larger = stronger LoRA effect. Usually equal to rank; when rs_lora is enabled, often set to √rank
descriptions.lokr_factor: LoKr factorization factor: larger = stronger compression, fewer parameters; smaller = more parameters. Default 8 suits most cases
descriptions.lora_dora: DoRA: trains weight direction and magnitude separately; usually more stable convergence at slight VRAM cost
descriptions.lora_rs: rs-LoRA: scale=α/√r instead of α/r, more stable at high rank (>32)
descriptions.lora_dropout: Dropout on LoRA input features: larger = stronger regularization but slower convergence; 0 = disabled
descriptions.lora_rank_dropout: Dropout on LoRA's rank dimensions (a subset of rank dims is randomly active each step): larger = stronger regularization; 0 = disabled
descriptions.lora_module_dropout: Probability of skipping the entire LoRA module (stochastic depth): the module contributes nothing on that step; larger = stronger regularization; 0 = disabled
descriptions.grad_accum: Gradient accumulation steps (effective batch = batch_size × grad_accum)
descriptions.learning_rate: Learning rate. Automagic recommends 1e-6 (auto-applied on switch); Lion recommends AdamW lr / 3; Prodigy / PPSF require 1.0
descriptions.lr_scheduler: Learning-rate scheduler (none = constant; Prodigy / PPSF lock this to none)
descriptions.lr_scheduler_t_mult: cosine_with_restart cycle-length multiplier relative to the previous cycle (>1 = lengthening cycles)
descriptions.lr_scheduler_eta_min: LR decay floor for cosine schedules; should be much smaller than the initial lr (e.g. 1e-6 for an initial lr of 1e-4)
descriptions.optimizer_type: Optimizer. adamw is the baseline; automagic adapts per-parameter lr (recommend lr=1e-6); lion uses ~half the state of AdamW (recommend lr = AdamW lr / 3); prodigy / prodigy_plus_schedulefree self-estimate lr (fill 1.0)
descriptions.prodigy_d_coef: Overall scaling coefficient on Prodigy's estimated d; larger = larger effective lr. Raise (2.0+) when underfitting; lower (0.5) when overfitting or on small datasets
descriptions.prodigy_safeguard_warmup: Prevent d from being inflated by early high gradients during Prodigy warmup; default on for stability
descriptions.ppsf_d_coef: PPSF d scaling coefficient (0.5 for small datasets, 2.0 for overfitting)
descriptions.ppsf_prodigy_steps: Freeze d after N PPSF steps (0 = never freeze; recommended 1/4 to 1/2 of total steps)
descriptions.ppsf_beta2: PPSF Adam β2 (PPSF default 0.99, better for small epochs than AdamW's 0.999)
descriptions.ppsf_split_groups: Estimate d per param group in PPSF (recommended)
descriptions.ppsf_split_groups_mean: Average group d values when PPSF split_groups is enabled (recommended off for LoRA multi-param groups)
descriptions.ppsf_use_speed: PPSF speed mode (experimental, may be unstable)
descriptions.ppsf_fused_back_pass: Integrate PPSF with fused backward (can significantly reduce VRAM pressure)
descriptions.ppsf_use_stableadamw: Enable stable-AdamW style normalization in PPSF to prevent abnormal single-step gradient scale; default on
descriptions.weight_decay: Weight decay: larger = stronger penalty on weight magnitude, helps with overfitting; 0 = disabled, typical 0.001-0.1, too large will destabilize training
descriptions.kv_trim: Cross-attention KV trim: crop to nearest bucket (64/128/256/512) by actual token count to reduce padding compute
descriptions.noise_enhancement_type: Noise augmentation (default none). offset adds per-sample DC bias to noise; pyramid stacks low-frequency noise at multiple scales. Both alter low-frequency components through different mechanisms; mutually exclusive to prevent stacking. LoRA training defaults to none
descriptions.noise_offset: DC bias strength (0-0.2, 0=off). Shifts noise mean away from 0, giving the model exposure to extreme brightness scenes (pure black / pure white / high contrast). Typical range 0.05-0.1; below 0.05 the noise is nearly identical to baseline, above 0.1 the initial loss rises substantially
descriptions.pyramid_noise_iters: Number of pyramid noise levels (0-6, 0=off). Each level injects at spatial // 2^(k+1). Effective strength is controlled by pyramid_noise_discount — iters alone determines which frequency bands are covered; layer count makes little difference when discount is low
descriptions.pyramid_noise_discount: Per-level decay coefficient (0.1-0.9). Key knob for low-frequency strength: anima normalizes overall noise std to 1, so discount sets the low-frequency share. 0.1-0.4 leaves noise close to standard Gaussian after normalization (equivalent to off); 0.5-0.7 significantly alters low-frequency structure
descriptions.timestep_sampling: Sampling distribution. logit_normal biases the middle (SD3/Anima default); uniform is equal probability; mode is a single-peak shift; mixed_* blends uniform with a biased end (ratio controlled by timestep_mix_low_prob)
descriptions.timestep_shift: Internal distribution shift for logit-normal / mode: >1 biases the high-noise end (coarse structure), <1 biases the low-noise end (details)
descriptions.timestep_mix_low_prob: Ratio of samples sent to the biased end in mixed_* modes: 0 = all uniform; typical 0.15-0.30
descriptions.schedule_shift: Extra σ schedule shift applied to t after sampling: 1.0 = no shift; larger = biased toward high-noise end overall. Differs from timestep_shift: acts on the final t rather than inside logit-normal
descriptions.infonoise_enabled: [InfoNoise] Enable adaptive timestep sampling: dynamically adjusts the t distribution during training based on information content, focusing on more useful training intervals
descriptions.infonoise_K: [InfoNoise] Number of log-σ bins (16-256): larger = finer resolution but sparser samples per bin
descriptions.infonoise_N_warm: [InfoNoise] Warmup steps: 0 = automatic 1/5 of total steps (minimum 200)
descriptions.infonoise_M: [InfoNoise] Sampling distribution refresh period: recompute every M steps. Larger = less compute overhead but slower distribution updates
descriptions.infonoise_B: [InfoNoise] FIFO buffer capacity per bin: larger = smoother averaging but slower response
descriptions.infonoise_beta: [InfoNoise] How strongly the adaptive distribution responds to the latest batch (0.1-0.999): 0.9 means new samples carry 90% weight and the previous estimate 10%. Larger = faster response but more variance. The default 0.9 already pairs with a 256-step FIFO that provides underlying smoothing; rarely needs tuning
descriptions.infonoise_N_min: [InfoNoise] Refresh trigger condition: minimum samples per bin required before recomputing the distribution. Must be ≤ infonoise_B
descriptions.infonoise_gate_pivot_c: [InfoNoise] Gate function pivot c: controls how aggressively low-noise regions are suppressed. Default 0.15 (paper §5 CIFAR reported value) is robust across datasets; set 0 to use dynamic selection (paper Eq 87 literal implementation), other positive values to override. Usually keep the default
descriptions.loss_type: Training loss type. mse classic; huber is robust to outliers (quadratic when |x|<δ, linear when |x|≥δ)
descriptions.huber_c: [Huber loss] Delta coefficient (quadratic/linear transition point): larger = closer to MSE, smaller = more outlier-tolerant. Typical 0.1-0.3
descriptions.loss_weighting: Loss weighting scheme: none = no weighting; min_snr suppresses weights on easy steps; detail_inv_t boosts low-t detail steps; cosmap uses SD3-style cosine mapping
descriptions.min_snr_gamma: Min-SNR threshold: caps the weight of high-SNR easy steps (low-t end). Default 5.0; smaller = stronger suppression
descriptions.weight_cap_ratio: Batch max/min weight ratio cap: limits extreme weight contributions. 0 = disabled; recommended 5 for small batch + Prodigy
descriptions.detail_inv_t_min: detail_inv_t weight lower bound. Default 1.0; raising to 1.5 lets high-t steps get a slight boost (<1.0 has no effect since 1/t≥1 always holds)
descriptions.detail_inv_t_max: detail_inv_t weight upper bound. Default 5.0; lower (e.g. 3) weakens detail emphasis, higher (e.g. 8) strengthens it aggressively
descriptions.grad_clip_max_norm: Max gradient norm: when the step's total gradient norm exceeds this value, rescale it down to this value to prevent a single outlier step from blowing up the model. Default 1.0 fits most cases; lower to 0.5 if bf16+DoRA/LoKr is unstable; 0 disables.
descriptions.mixed_precision: Training precision. bf16 recommended (same dynamic range as fp32, stable); fp16 has the same memory footprint but smaller dynamic range and is prone to gradient overflow; no = fp32, most stable but doubles VRAM
descriptions.attention_backend: Attention backend. none = PyTorch SDPA (default fallback); xformers saves more VRAM; flash_attn is fastest (requires Ampere+ GPU)
descriptions.num_workers: Data loading worker threads; larger = faster loading but more RAM usage. Must be 0 on Windows
descriptions.save_state_every_steps: Save full training state every N steps (resume checkpoint, 0 disables)
descriptions.save_state_every_epochs: Save full training state every N epochs (resume checkpoint, 0 disables)
descriptions.resume_lora: Continue training from an existing LoRA (loads weights only)
descriptions.resume_state: Resume from training state (full checkpoint resume)
descriptions.sample_prompt: Single prompt mode: all sample images during training share this prompt (ignored when sample_prompts is set)
descriptions.sample_prompts: Multiple prompt rotation (takes priority over sample_prompt)
descriptions.wandb_notice: ⚠️ Do not fill in these settings unless you know what you are doing
descriptions.wandb_enabled: Preset-level WandB toggle (blank = use global settings)
descriptions.wandb_project: WandB project name (blank = use global settings)
descriptions.wandb_entity: WandB team/user name (blank = use global settings)
descriptions.wandb_base_url: WandB custom server URL (blank = use global settings)
descriptions.wandb_log_samples: Preset-level sample logging toggle (blank = use global settings)
descriptions.wandb_sample_max_side: Sample max side (0 = use global settings)
descriptions.wandb_upload_model: Preset-level model upload toggle (blank = use global settings)
descriptions.wandb_upload_model_policy: Model artifact retention (all = keep all, last = keep latest only)
descriptions.wandb_upload_state_manual: Preset-level manual training state upload toggle (blank = use global settings)
descriptions.wandb_upload_state_manual_policy: Manual training state artifact retention policy
descriptions.wandb_upload_state_auto: Preset-level auto training state upload toggle (blank = use global settings)
descriptions.wandb_upload_state_auto_policy: Auto training state artifact retention policy

altDescriptions.timestep_sampling: [Timestep sampling] Distribution; when InfoNoise is enabled this is the warmup baseline, then adaptive CDF takes over
altDescriptions.timestep_shift: [InfoNoise warmup] Baseline shift during warmup when InfoNoise is enabled; adaptive CDF takes over afterward
altDescriptions.timestep_mix_low_prob: [InfoNoise warmup] Mix ratio during warmup when InfoNoise is enabled with a mixed_* baseline; adaptive CDF takes over afterward
altDescriptions.schedule_shift: [InfoNoise warmup] Only applies during warmup when InfoNoise is enabled; adaptive CDF takes over afterward

tagEditor.addPlaceholder: Add a tag, then press Enter / comma
tagEditor.textPlaceholder: Comma / newline separated, auto-sync on blur

llmWorkspace.resetBuiltinTitle: Reset this built-in preset to the program default
llmWorkspace.saveAsTitle: Copy the current preset fields into a new preset
llmWorkspace.imageSizeHint: Claude and similar services limit images to <limit>5 MB</limit> each. Larger images will be compressed below this value.
llmWorkspace.responsesWarning: ⚠️ Responses endpoint only uses system + first user message; other messages are ignored
llmWorkspace.secretSavedPlaceholder: Saved (hidden); enter a new value to overwrite

llmMessages.imageHint: Image is inserted here during tagging · draggable
llmMessages.imageReplaceHint: Replaced with the image being tagged each time
llmMessages.placeholderSystem: System prompt, e.g. You are an image captioning assistant...
llmMessages.placeholderUser: (Optional) user content — few-shot example / task description

projects.title: Projects
projects.description: Each project targets one LoRA training goal — character, style, or concept. Create a project to start the pipeline.
projects.importZipHint: Upload a browser-local file or choose a server path to import a bundle zip
projects.importBundleHint: Choose where the bundle zip comes from: browser upload or server-local path.
projects.importUploadHint: Choose a bundle zip from this computer.
projects.importPathHint: Use PathPicker to select a zip visible to the Studio server.
projects.noProjectsHint: Click "New project" to start a LoRA training
projects.deleteConfirm:
Delete project "{{title}}" ({{slug}})?
This includes all versions, datasets, and training outputs. This cannot be undone.
projects.confirmEmptyTrash: Permanently delete all trashed projects? This cannot be undone.

queue.title: Queue
queue.description: One task at a time · Auto-start next on completion
queue.cancelHint: Send cancel signal to stop the running task
queue.emptyHint: Queue tasks from the project training page
queue.pauseHint: Save state — resume later from the same step
queue.resumeHint: Continue training from the saved checkpoint (frozen config)
queue.cancelPausedHint: Abandon this task and delete pause files
queue.heldBanner: Queue is on hold; pending tasks won't auto-start

pauseProgress.title: Pausing #{{id}}
pauseProgress.saved: Paused at step {{step}} — resume any time
pauseProgress.timeoutTitle: Save is taking longer than expected
pauseProgress.timeoutDesc: {{n}}s elapsed. You can wait, or force-terminate the task.
pauseProgress.forceCancelKeepHint: Terminate the child now; mark paused if .pt landed, otherwise canceled
pauseProgress.failedDesc: Child process exited unexpectedly (exit code {{code}}). View logs or terminate.

holdModal.title: Hold queue?
holdModal.descNoRunning: Pending tasks won't auto-start. Any running task is unaffected.
holdModal.optionPauseToo: Also pause task #{{id}} (saves progress; resume manually or with release)

pauseConfirm.title: Pause training?
pauseConfirm.adaptiveWarning: Some experimental parameters (InfoNoise adaptive sampler, Prodigy-family adaptive optimisers, cosine LR scheduler) may show quality fluctuation after pause/resume.
pauseConfirm.epochLoss: Resume will continue from the end of the previous epoch; progress in the current epoch will be discarded.

queue.resumeFailedMissing: Pause file is missing — cannot resume. Start a fresh task from another .pt via ResumeFieldPicker.

queueDetail.deleteNote: LoRA / training logs / monitor files will remain on disk.
queueDetail.noProjectAssoc: Task has no project/version association, output directory not found
queueDetail.compressionTimeout: Compression timeout (60s), ignore if download started

settings.closeDirtyConfirm: Closing the drawer will discard unsaved changes. Close anyway?

proxy.enableDesc: When enabled, Booru scraping requests go through the proxy configured below. Only covers the Booru download path; model downloads (HuggingFace / ModelScope) do not use this proxy.
proxy.httpsDesc: Usually the same as HTTP proxy
proxy.noProxyDesc: Comma-separated hostnames or IPs, e.g. localhost,127.0.0.1,.internal.com
proxy.tips1: Proxy configuration takes effect immediately after saving, no service restart required.
proxy.tips2: If proxy requires authentication, use the format http://user:pass@host:port.
proxy.tips3: Leaving a proxy type (e.g., HTTPS) empty will fall back to using the HTTP proxy address.

tagDictionary.title: Tag translation dictionary
tagDictionary.empty: Not initialized — click 「Restore default」 below to retry
tagDictionary.uploadHint: CSV / TXT format: one line per `english_tag,zh1 zh2`. Replaces (not merges) the current dictionary. Max 10MB / 200k entries.
tagDictionary.showToggleHint: Display English + Chinese on tag chips. UI-only — does not modify caption data.

settings.title: Settings
settings.danbooruUsernamePlaceholder: Required — Danbooru no longer supports anonymous access after Cloudflare
settings.batchSizeHint: Images per GPU inference batch; CPU automatically falls back to 1
settings.downloadSourceHelp: ModelScope is faster for users in China; unmapped models fall back to HuggingFace automatically.
settings.hfTokenHelp: For HF private repo authentication. Public repos (including SmilingWolf WD14 / cella110n CLTagger) do not need it.
settings.hfEndpointHelp: Model download endpoint. Defaults to HuggingFace official. Pick "Custom URL" to paste a mirror / self-hosted reverse proxy.
settings.modelscopeTokenHelp: Public models do not need it; private repos or rate limits may require it.
settings.modelscopeInstallHelp: Install the CLI first with pip install modelscope before use.
settings.allowGpuDuringTrainHint: WD14 tagging with onnxruntime-gpu uses about ~2 GB; only enable if enough VRAM remains outside training, otherwise OOM may occur
settings.enableWandbHint: When enabled, all training tasks write to W&B; no longer occupies training config fields
settings.wandbEntityHint: Optional; team/user name, blank uses the default wandb account
settings.wandbBaseUrlHint: Optional; use for private W&B/self-hosted instances
settings.logSamplesHelp: When enabled, training sample images upload to public wandb.ai; disable for private IP / NSFW datasets and keep metrics only.
settings.sampleMaxSideHelp: Resize to this pixel size before upload. Source images are often 2K+; 1216 is enough for wandb browsing.
settings.sampleEveryNStepsHelp: 0 = no extra throttle. > 0 uploads only when global_step % N == 0, avoiding GB-scale images in long training runs.
settings.uploadModelHelp: Automatically upload model to W&B Artifacts when saved during training.
settings.uploadStateManualHelp: Upload manually saved training states (save_state_every_steps / epochs) to W&B Artifacts.
settings.uploadStateAutoHelp: Upload auto-saved per-epoch training state to W&B Artifacts.
settings.excludeTagsHelp: Automatically appends -tag when searching; applies to Gelbooru / Danbooru too.
settings.wd14CandidateHelp: {{desc}}. Select it as the current model_id; download missing versions.
settings.autoSyncPathsHelp: ON (default / most users): when forking a preset to a version, the 4 model path fields (transformer/vae/text_encoder/t5_tokenizer) are auto-overwritten from Settings; these 4 fields are disabled in preset/project pages. OFF (custom-model users): fork respects the absolute paths stored in the preset; the 4 fields become editable with a picker.
settings.defaultTransformerHelp: The selected version becomes the default transformer for new versions.
settings.upscalersHelpPath: Preprocess uses upscaling for small images. Files are stored in {{path}}.
settings.upscalersHelpDefault: The selected radio item is the default model used by the preprocess page. custom comes from the form below or previous custom downloads.
settings.customUpscalerHelpTypes: Download any .pth / .safetensors upscaler from HuggingFace or ModelScope.
settings.customUpscalerHelpEnable: After download it appears in the list above (marked custom); select it to enable.
settings.downloadRequiredFirst: Not downloaded yet; download it first
settings.packageInstalledRestart: Installed {{pkg}}=={{version}}; restart Studio to apply EP changes
settings.onnxRestartRequired: A new onnxruntime package is installed, but the current process is still using the old one. Restart Studio to apply EP changes.
settings.hfCustomHint: Paste self-hosted proxy / sjtug / Tencent mirror, etc.

upscalerDescriptions.animeSharp: Anime line art / flat color friendly (Kim2091, ESRGAN-RRDB)

settings.confirmInstallOnnxAuto:
Platform + nvidia-smi detection chooses the best package (Windows + GPU → DirectML; Linux + GPU → CUDA; no GPU → CPU). Package installation takes a few minutes.

Note: restart Studio after installation for it to take effect. Continue?
settings.confirmInstallOnnxGpu:
The current onnxruntime package will be removed and onnxruntime-gpu (CUDA EP) installed. Package installation takes a few minutes.

Note: restart Studio after installation for it to take effect. Continue?
settings.confirmInstallOnnxCpu:
The current onnxruntime package will be removed and onnxruntime (CPU) installed. Package installation takes a few minutes.

Note: restart Studio after installation for it to take effect. Continue?
settings.confirmInstallOnnxDirectml:
The current onnxruntime package will be removed and onnxruntime-directml (DirectML EP, DX12, vendor-agnostic) installed. Package installation takes a few minutes.

Note: restart Studio after installation for it to take effect. Continue?
settings.gpuRunningCpuEp: GPU present but using CPU EP
settings.onnxCpuEpWarning: NVIDIA GPU detected, but onnxruntime only has CPU EP — WD14 / CLTagger will be very slow. Expand “Candidates” to install DirectML (recommended on Windows, vendor-agnostic) or the CUDA package.
settings.onnxNotInstalledHintGpu: Not installed. Click “Auto-detect + install” below to install the best package for your platform (Windows + GPU → DirectML; Linux + GPU → CUDA).
settings.onnxNotInstalledHintCpu: Not installed. Click “Auto-detect + install” below to install the CPU package (no NVIDIA GPU detected).
settings.cudaEpFailedCpu: CUDA EP failed to load and fell back to CPU.
settings.directmlWinOnlyHint: Windows only (no PyPI wheel for Linux/macOS)
settings.cudaPackageHint: NVIDIA GPU; best on Linux. On Windows, switch to DirectML if it silently falls back to CPU.
settings.directmlPackageHint: DX12 backend, bypasses CUDA/cuDNN compatibility issues, vendor-agnostic (NVIDIA/AMD/Intel).
settings.cpuPackageHint: Fallback; use when no GPU or the other two cannot install.
settings.confirmRegisterTorch:
A torch reinstall request will be registered ({{tag}} build).
After submitting, press Ctrl+C to close Studio and rerun studio.bat — torch will be installed on startup (~3 GB, 5-30 minutes). Continue?
settings.torchCpuWithGpuWarning: NVIDIA GPU detected, but the current torch is the CPU build — training / generation will run on CPU and each step can take tens of seconds. Use the button below to reinstall the {{tag}} build.
settings.torchCudaUnavailableWarning: torch is a CUDA build but torch.cuda.is_available()=False. This usually cannot be fixed by pip (driver too old / WSL lacks CUDA support / container has no GPU passthrough). Check the NVIDIA driver and CUDA Toolkit.
settings.noNvidiaDriverCannotCuda: No NVIDIA driver detected; CUDA build cannot be installed
settings.manualCudaHint: Choose based on your driver / preference; auto is usually fine. The wrong version may fail on import and require choosing again.
settings.installCpuBuildHint: Install CPU build (very slow; diagnostics / no GPU only)
settings.confirmInstallFlashAuto:
The best matching flash_attn wheel will be selected from GitHub Releases and installed.
Package installation takes a few minutes; restart Studio after installation. Continue?
settings.confirmInstallFlashUrl:
This wheel will be installed with pip. Package installation takes a few minutes.
Restart Studio after installation for it to take effect. Continue?
settings.githubApiFailed: GitHub API request failed (network may be unstable in China; refresh and retry):
settings.noWheelForPython: No prebuilt wheel was found for {{python}} (the current Python version may not be supported yet). Select another candidate below manually, or paste a URL from GitHub Releases.
settings.wheelQueryFailed: Query failed or no match (check network connection)
settings.wheelAbiIncompatible: Python ABI is incompatible; force install may fail
settings.confirmInstallXformers:
xformers will be installed with pip using the PyTorch index URL matching the current torch+cu.
Installation can take several to tens of minutes; restart Studio after installation. Continue?
settings.xformersSubtitle: attention acceleration (mutually exclusive with Flash Attention)
settings.xformersHelp1: xformers and Flash Attention are mutually exclusive; each training / generation task chooses one attention_backend value (none / xformers / flash_attn).
settings.xformersHelp2: xformers is more broadly compatible (sm_70+), while flash_attn is faster (sm_80+ Ampere and newer).
settings.xformersHelp3: Install failures usually mean upstream PyPI / PyTorch index has no wheel for this torch+cu combination (common for 5090 / new GPUs). The button toast shows the tail of stderr; use it to downgrade torch or wait for upstream coverage.
settings.taeFluxHelp: The TAEFlux decode model is downloaded in the background when the server starts; no separate UI action is needed.

idleTimeout.title: Auto-unload VRAM
idleTimeout.label: Unload after idle (minutes)
idleTimeout.help: The generate daemon keeps the model in VRAM after the first run so you can quickly retry with different params. If no new generation arrives within this window, the model is auto-unloaded to free VRAM (next run reloads, ~30-60s). 0 = off, keep the model resident until you manually click "Free VRAM". Recommended: 10 minutes.
idleTimeout.offHint: Off: model stays resident until manually freed

settings.densityTightHelp: Compact: smaller text and tighter spacing for small screens or dense information
settings.densityDefaultHelp: Default: standard text size and spacing
settings.densityLooseHelp: Comfortable: larger text and wider spacing for readability
settings.operationTimeout: {{label}} timed out ({{mins}} minutes). Check terminal output and refresh manually.
settings.versionInfo1: Automatic checks run every 24 hours and only watch master; dev must be triggered manually and does not affect the Topbar red dot
settings.versionInfo2: Update / switch uses git reset --hard underneath and runs pip / npm install when needed
settings.versionInfo3: Running tasks / dirty local working tree → action is rejected by pre-flight
settings.versionInfo4: master shows release tags; dev shows the commit timeline and can switch to any commit
settings.onboardingReopenHelp: Re-open the first-run install panel. Useful for checking what's not installed yet, or for filling in what you skipped.
settings.serviceRestartHelp1: Shut down and restart the backend process.
settings.serviceRestartHelp2: Common use cases: force refresh after editing secrets.json, or apply a newly installed onnxruntime / PyTorch EP.
settings.confirmRestartService:
The Studio backend service will be shut down and restarted.

This usually takes 30 seconds to 1 minute. The web UI will be unavailable during restart, and the page will wait and refresh automatically.

If training / tagging tasks are running, the request will be rejected (protected since PR-B).
settings.taskRunningCancelFirst: Tasks are running; cancel them first: {{names}}
settings.dirtyWorkingTree: Local uncommitted changes exist; commit or stash first
settings.noRollbackTarget: No rollback target (first startup / .last_version was cleaned)
settings.preflightInfo: git reset --hard · stop server · run pip/npm install if needed · restart · estimated 1-3 minutes
settings.progressWaitReload: The page will wait and refresh automatically
settings.progressDetail: The current process has sent SIGINT and exited; cli.py is running git reset / pip / npm / restart. Realtime progress is unavailable because the server is down. The frontend will reconnect and refresh after restart; failures appear as a red banner on the master card.
settings.releaseNoEntry: This tag has no entry in release_notes.yaml
settings.topbarMasterOnly: Topbar red dot + automatic checks only watch this channel.
settings.switchToDevHeadDesc: Switch to dev HEAD (git reset --hard origin/dev)
settings.fetchDevHint: Click [Fetch dev] to view recent commits.
settings.fetchFailedCached: ⚠ fetch failed ({{error}}); list is local origin/dev cache
settings.sensitiveSavedPlaceholder: Saved (hidden); enter a new value to overwrite
settings.gitInitEnabled: Git repository initialized; auto-update is enabled
settings.stableAhead: Local install is ahead of stable (possibly rollback / pre-release)
settings.stableAheadShort: Local install is ahead of stable
settings.stableDetached: Current commit is not in stable history
settings.devAheadShort: Local install is ahead of dev HEAD
settings.devDetached: Current commit is not in dev history
settings.gitRequiredHelp: Auto-update requires git installed locally. Install git first, then restart Studio.
settings.zipInstallGitInitHelp: This looks like a zip extraction install. To use one-click updates / channel switching in the version panel, initialize a local git repository first (about 30–60 MB of history metadata on first run). Local source will sync to upstream {{version}}, and local edits in the zip directory will be overwritten (npm-generated lockfiles / custom configs and similar files reset to upstream).
settings.channelUiOnly: UI view only; git is unchanged
settings.versionInfoChannel: Switching channels only changes the UI preference (git is unchanged); the actual “Switch to dev HEAD” / “Update to vX.Y.Z” actions are separate buttons
settings.versionInfoAutoCheck: Automatic checks run every 24 hours and only watch the stable channel; switching to the dev channel does not affect the Topbar red dot
settings.versionInfoUpdateImpl: Update / switch uses git reset --hard underneath and runs pip / npm install when needed
settings.versionInfoPreflight: Running tasks / dirty local working tree → action is rejected by pre-flight
settings.devAhead: Local install is ahead of dev HEAD (rare; possibly pre-release)

generate.title: Generate
generate.subtitle: Standalone inference · Single / XY matrix / two-image compare (outputs are temporary and disappear when the page closes)
generate.promptOrDatasetRequired: Enter at least one prompt, or choose a caption from the training set
generate.xySinglePromptOnly: XY mode only supports one prompt (multiple prompts and XY are mutually exclusive)
generate.loraHint: Pick versions from projects and tune weight with one slider
generate.pickFromDatasetTitle: Pick a caption from the training set as the prompt
generate.seedHint: Specific number → reproducible; 0 → random each time
generate.blockedByActiveTask: Queue task #{{id}} is using the GPU — wait for it to finish or cancel before generating (running both will fight for VRAM)
generate.originalReleasedCoverOnly: Original image was released; only the cover thumbnail is available
generate.originalReleasedThumbOnly: Original image was released; only the thumbnail is available
generate.vramAlreadyFree: VRAM is already free (model not loaded)
generate.daemonNoModel: No model loaded; nothing to unload
generate.daemonUnloadTitle: Release VRAM used by the inference daemon; it reloads on demand next time
generate.clearDatasetPickTitle: Clear the selected caption; closing the panel keeps it selected
generate.closeDatasetPickerTitle: Close panel and keep the selection
generate.selectedDatasetTagsLabel: Selected tags (appended after the positive prompt when generating)
generate.selectedDatasetTagsPlaceholder: No caption selected — click a row above to activate one
generate.pickExternalTitle: Pick any .safetensors file from the system
generate.noTrainedLora: No trained LoRA yet — train one first, or use “External file”
generate.axisValueInvalidNumber: {{axis}} value “{{value}}” is not a valid number
generate.axisLoraMissing: The LoRA #{{n}} bound to {{axis}} does not exist
generate.noCkptFiles: No ckpt files found for this LoRA (*.safetensors under output/ required)
generate.removeYAxisTitle: Remove Y axis (fall back to single axis)
generate.externalLoraNoCkpt: External LoRA files cannot list ckpts (pick a project LoRA from the picker)
generate.xyGridHelp: Wheel to zoom · drag to pan · Ctrl+click to select · double-click fullscreen
generate.exportPngTitle: Merge the whole XY matrix and axis labels into one PNG download
generate.selectedSamplesUnavailable: Selected samples are unavailable — pick 2 images in the grid again
generate.imageCachePending: Image is being generated… (cache is not ready yet; wait for the next step progress)
generate.pruneStaleTitle: Check and delete stale history whose originals were released by the server
generate.allCellsLoadFailed: All cell images failed to load (the originals may have been released)

overview.subtitle: {{n}} downloads · {{v}} versions
overview.tasksPlaceholder: Training tasks for this project (to be implemented)
overview.outputPlaceholder: LoRA artifacts produced by this project (to be implemented)
overview.tasksEmpty: No training tasks for this project yet

detail.emptyCurate: Pick training images in ③ Curate first
detail.emptyTag: Generate captions in ④ Tag first
detail.emptyResolution: See details in ② Preprocess → Upscale
detail.emptyAspect: See bucket stats in ② Preprocess → Crop
detail.emptyReg: Generate reg set or explicitly skip in ⑥ Reg
detail.regEmptyHint: Not generated · skip phase in ⑥

banner.preparingSub: Dataset preparation · training can start once finished
banner.smallerBatchHint: Opened new version · adjust batch_size in ⑦ Train then retrain
banner.noArtifact: No artifact file yet, cannot proceed

overview.outputEmptyVersion: This version has no LoRA artifact yet

snapshot.title: Training config snapshot
snapshot.subtitle: Config frozen when this task started. Captions / images are not part of the snapshot.
snapshot.applyConfirmDesc: Write this config back to the current version's training config (overwrites the existing one), then jump to the train page to edit / start training.
snapshot.empty: No config snapshot for this task
snapshot.notFoundHint: May be an old task from before v8 schema; snapshot was not created.
snapshot.noVersionLink: This task is not linked to a version; cannot apply

layout.deleteVersionConfirm: Delete version "{{label}}"? This includes train/reg/output/samples and cannot be undone.
layout.labelInvalid: Label must only contain letters, numbers, underscores, hyphens, or dots
layout.forkNote: Copies train/, reg/, training config, and unlock state (output/, samples/ not copied)
layout.exportBundleConfigHint: (hyperparams for this version, no path fields)
layout.exportBundleDestinationHint: The bundle will be generated now, then downloaded by your browser or saved to data_exports/.
layout.exportBundleAtLeastOne: Select at least one item to export

tagEdit.title: Tag edit
tagEdit.subtitle: Batch edit tags · stage locally · write on save
tagEdit.downloadTitle: Download current version training dataset as zip
tagEdit.noVersion: Please select or create a version first
tagEdit.noImagesHint: No images yet. Complete the curation and tagging steps first.
tagEdit.triggerWordHint: Already written as the first token of every caption during Step 4 (Tagging). To change it, go back to Step 4, update the trigger word, and re-tag.
tagEdit.unsavedConfirmMessage: You have unsaved tag changes on {{n}} image(s). Navigating away will discard them. Leave anyway?

download.noResults: Query returned 0 results, nothing to download
download.confirmDelete:
Delete {{n}} images from download/ (including caption metadata)?
This cannot be undone.
download.emptyHint: No images yet — use Booru scraping or local upload above
download.uploadFromPathHint: Import an image or zip visible to the Studio server.

preprocess.customModelGoSettings: Custom model not found — go to Settings → Preprocess
preprocess.confirmRestore: Restore {{n}} processed images (delete preprocess/ copies, back to 'pending')?
preprocess.targetHintOff: Off: every image runs 4× model (high disk cost)
preprocess.customModelLocal: Custom model not local (go to Settings → Preprocess)
preprocess.upscaleSelectedHint: Select images in the list to enable
preprocess.smartHint: Smart: if pixels sufficient → skip model, use LANCZOS
preprocess.emptyForBin: No images in this resolution bin.
preprocess.restoreHintDisabled: Select processed images to enable — restore = delete preprocess copy
preprocess.mixedSelectionWarning: Selection has {{pending}} pending + {{processed}} processed; buttons act on each separately.
preprocess.emptyProcessed: No outputs yet — click 'Upscale all' above
preprocess.emptyAll: No images — go to Download first
preprocess.actionNote: 'Direct resize' uses LANCZOS (no model, fast). 'Upscale+resize' is the slow path.
preprocess.diskNoteOff: Target off: each 4× PNG is large — high disk cost.
preprocess.diskNoteSmart: Smart mode: resized to target area, typically 1–3 MB per PNG.
preprocess.vramNote: Larger tile = more VRAM; reduce to 128/192 if OOM.
preprocess.nextNote: Preprocess is optional — skipped images use originals, processed images use the preprocess copy. ADR 0004: user sees one image set.

preprocessOverview.subtitle: Upscale / crop / inpaint — Overview (preview + undo)
preprocessOverview.title: Processed images
preprocessOverview.empty: Nothing processed yet. Go to «Upscale» or «Crop» to start.
preprocessOverview.emptyAll: No images in this project yet. Download or upload some first.
preprocessOverview.emptyRemoved: No images marked removed by the duplicate review tool.
preprocessOverview.restoreSelectedTitle: Undo flag: processed → revert to download/; duplicate-removed → unhide
preprocessOverview.resetAllTitle: Delete every preprocess product and revert to download/ originals
preprocessOverview.confirmRestore: Undo {{n}}? Processed images delete their preprocess copy and revert to download/; duplicate-removed flags are cleared. Irreversible.
preprocessOverview.confirmResetAll: Undo all {{n}} images? All preprocess copies (upscale / crop / ...) deleted; everything reverts to download/. Irreversible.

preprocessCrop.subtitle: Upscale / crop / inpaint — Crop tool
preprocessCrop.hintFree: Drag on canvas to draw a free-AR crop
preprocessCrop.hintLocked: New crops locked to {{ar}}; resize keeps AR
preprocessCrop.clusterSectionDesc: Auto-group by aspect ratio and pre-fill one crop per image (tweak by hand after)
preprocessCrop.emptyHintLine1: Drag on the canvas to create the first crop.
preprocessCrop.emptyHintLine2: One image can have multiple crops (face + full body).
preprocessCrop.rrNote: Each rect = 1 PNG. N=1 overwrites the source; N>1 derives _c0 / _c1 ... copies. Cluster is just a prefill — rects stay editable.
preprocessCrop.toastNoCrops: No crops to submit (draw at least one rect)

tag.title: Auto tagging
tag.subtitle: WD14 / CLTagger local inference, or remote LLM vision tagging

reg.title: Reg set (optional)
reg.subtitle: Booru pull or base-model neutral prior; mirror train structure or flat single bucket 1_data/

train.title: Train
train.subtitle: Pick preset → Edit config → Queue training

curate.noVersion: Please select/create a version first (top-left VersionTabs)
curate.noTargetFolder: Please select a Train set group on the right first
curate.confirmDeleteFolder: Delete group {{name}}? This will clear {{n}} training copies (Dataset untouched)
curate.noFolderTitle: Select a Train set group on the right first
curate.downloadEmptyHint: All Dataset images used, or nothing imported yet
curate.trainNoFolder: Click a group chip above to switch view
curate.noTrainFolders: No training groups yet, click "+ New" to create one
curate.folderSwitchTitle: Click to switch view + add target
curate.previewHintLeft: ←/→ Browse · Enter/Space Add to Train set
curate.previewHintRight: ←/→ Browse · Delete/Backspace Remove from Train set

duplicates.title: Duplicate / Variant Review
duplicates.subtitle: Preprocess intervention: scan groups, review images, then mark skips
duplicates.advancedDesc: Collapsed by default; expand when tuning false positives on large datasets
duplicates.empty: Parameters stay above; concrete grouped images appear here after a scan.
duplicates.noGroups: Scanned {{total}} images and found no duplicate candidates.
duplicates.scanDone: Scan complete: {{groups}} groups, {{candidates}} duplicate candidates, {{blur}} blur candidates, {{crops}} crop relations
duplicates.confirmApply:
Mark {{n}} selected candidates as duplicate-removed?
Confirm you reviewed the concrete image groups on this page. This does not move or delete download/ files; later preprocess, curation, and training steps will skip them.
duplicates.previewHint: ←/→ Browse duplicate candidates · select before removing
duplicates.qualityHint: Quality candidates are reports only until you manually mark images for removal.
duplicates.cropEmpty: No obvious crop or scale relations found.

tag.paramsSubtitle: Pre-filled from {{link}} · Effective this run only
tag.llmSubtitle: OpenAI compatible · Switch preset to change full config
tag.llmNoPreset: No LLM preset available. Please configure in Settings.
tag.responsesWarning: ⚠️ Responses endpoint: only uses system + first user message; other messages ignored
tag.wd14Desc: WD14 ONNX local inference, no network needed
tag.cltaggerDesc: CLTagger ONNX local inference, supports character threshold
tag.llmDesc: OpenAI compatible vision LLM, supports Responses / Chat Completions
tag.triggerWordHint: Prepended as the first tag of every caption during tagging; empty = disabled. Changing this and starting tagging will persist it to the current version.
tag.onExistingHint: What to do when an image already has a caption file (.txt or .json).

presets.droppedFieldsBody: The following fields are not recognized in this version and were ignored:
presets.defaultedFieldsBody: The following fields had invalid values and were reset to defaults:
presets.nameInvalid: Only letters, digits, _, and - are allowed
presets.importConflictBody: Choose Overwrite to replace it with the uploaded contents, or enter a new name below and Save as.
presets.importOverwriteTitle: Replace the existing preset {{name}} with uploaded contents
presets.label: Preset
presets.exportPresetHint: Export the complete parameters currently in the form, either through the browser downloader or into data_exports/.
presets.exportDownloadHint: Save the current preset as YAML to this computer.
presets.exportDataExportsHint: Write the current preset YAML to the Studio server data_exports/ folder.
presets.descPlaceholder: Describe its purpose; shown on the training preset card…
presets.saveBeforeDownload: Save the preset first; download streams the on-disk yaml from the server.

train.confirmForkPreset: Switching presets will overwrite the current version config (saved changes will be lost). Continue?
train.promptPresetName: Preset name (project-specific fields like data_dir will be stripped)
train.createdPreset: Created preset {{name}} and applied to current version
train.customizedTitle: This version is based on a preset copy. Changes only affect this version.
train.saveAsPresetTitle: Save current version config as a global preset
train.noConfigHint: Click + New preset on the left to auto-generate one with schema defaults + project model paths and apply it, or pick an existing preset.
train.resetToGlobalDefaultTitle: Replace with current value computed from Settings → Model management
train.confirmReset: Reset config to preset {{name}}? All customizations on the current version will be lost.
train.pickerTitleConfigured: Current version has its own config; click to pick a preset as a starting point to reset
train.pickerTitleEmpty: Click to pick a preset as a starting point and create the current version's config
train.notConfiguredLabel: Not configured — pick a preset to start

reg.noTrainForAi: No train images yet, please complete Step 1 (download) or Step 2 (curate) first
reg.noTrainForBuild: No train images yet, go to ① Curate / ② Download first
reg.confirmDelete: Delete the current reg set? This is irreversible (meta + all images will be removed).
reg.sourceAiHint: Aligned with the DreamBooth paper; first launch downloads the base model and uses the GPU.
reg.sourceBooruHint: Simpler and faster, no GPU; reg set drifts toward booru average. Exclude tags are shared with AI prior — edits sync both ways.
reg.modeHintAi: What to do with existing images on re-run
reg.modeHintBooru: What to do with existing images on re-scrape
reg.targetMirrorLocked: Mirror structure scrapes per-subfolder by train actual count, {{n}} total
reg.autoTagKindTitle: Tagger to use (WD14 local / CLTagger local)
reg.autoDedupTitle: After build: scan duplicates → keep 1 per group → incremental top-up if short. Up to 3 rounds. Runs before resolution clustering.
reg.modeTitle: Incremental: keep existing images, top up the gap + exclude deleted. Full: wipe reg/ (including .deleted_ids.json).
reg.buildModeTitle: Flat: all images go to 1_data/, with target count. Mirror: mirror train subfolders (5_concept/, 1_general/, ...).
reg.buildModeLocked: Current reg set has '{{mode}}' structure; clear it before switching, or pick Full mode to wipe along the way.
reg.targetCountTitle: Target image count; blank = use train total ({{fallback}}). Flat mode only.
reg.emptyRegHint: Configure in "Settings & Logs" then click "Start generation" in the top right
reg.statusNotExist: Current version reg set: does not exist
reg.clusteringNoneTitle: Resolution variance too large or disabled — training uses bucketing instead
reg.skipSimilarTitle: Use only even-index candidates to avoid adjacent similar images (default ✓)
reg.maxCropTitle: Maximum crop ratio within one cluster (default 0.1 = 10%)
reg.aiDescription: Use the base model to generate counterpart images from each train image tag as the reg set. Training sees the base distribution too, pushing concepts that should not be learned back to the base. Each train image (<count>{{n}}</count> total) generates 1 reg image written to <path>reg/{subfolder}/</path>. Excluded tags are shared with the left Booru panel and stay synced.
reg.incrementalLabel: Top-up mode (skip files already in reg subfolder with matching prefix)
reg.excludeNoTags: No tag distribution in train yet. You can use custom excludes below.
reg.excludePlaceholder: Enter tag, press Enter to add
reg.regPreviewTitle: reg/ ({{n}} images) — click to preview; checkbox / Shift·Ctrl click for multi-select
reg.deleteFilesTitle: Delete the selected images (with .txt captions); incremental top-up won't re-fetch them
reg.confirmDeleteFiles: Delete {{n}} selected images? Their booru IDs will be written to .deleted_ids.json and excluded on next "top-up" (irreversible).
reg.dedupTitle: Scan duplicates; auto-delete the suggested item in each group (no review panel). Synchronous — slow on large sets.
reg.confirmDedup: Run auto-dedup on the reg set? Keep 1 image per group, delete the rest (booru IDs written to .deleted_ids.json and excluded on next top-up). Irreversible.

onboarding.title: Welcome to AnimaLoraStudio
onboarding.subtitle: Here are the basics you'll want set up. Tick what you need and install them in one go. Already-installed items are auto-checked.
onboarding.downloadSourceHint: Pre-selected based on your language; feel free to change. This source is used for all model downloads.
onboarding.downloadSourceInstallHint: Switching applies to downloads not yet started — current in-flight downloads keep running. To switch right now, close this guide and restart Studio.
onboarding.skipWhileInstalling: Guide closed — downloads keep running in the background. To stop them, restart Studio.
onboarding.restartHint: All installs done — a service restart is required to take effect

base.label: Base model bundle
base.description: Anima main + VAE + Qwen3 + T5 — the core models needed for training (about 30 GB total)

tagger.label: Auto-tagging
tagger.description: WD14 model + ONNX Runtime — auto-generates tags for your training set

accel.label: Training acceleration (optional)
accel.description: Flash Attention — noticeable training speedup; skip on CPU / older GPUs where it won't build
